title: Lithospermum molle
intro: Lithospermum molle, also known as soft gromwell or soft-lithosperm, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small blue flowers and hairy, grayish-green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum molle include lithospermic acid, lithospermol, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Lithospermum molle is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Lithospermum molle is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It belongs to the genus Lithospermum, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to various parts of the world, including Europe, Asia, and North America. The Boraginaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are known for their diverse range of species, many of which have medicinal or ornamental value. Within the Boraginaceae family, Lithospermum molle is classified as a type of herbaceous perennial plant. The exact classification of Lithospermum molle can also be broken down further into more specific taxonomic categories, including its kingdom, phylum, class, order, and family.

Its root and leaves are used in various treatments, including those for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that Lithospermum molle may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which could contribute to its medicinal applications.
